steps.title: Identify the structural turning point
steps.content: The word “But” marks the passage's shift from presenting a widespread belief (and explaining it) to introducing evidence that contradicts it.

steps.title: Match the answer
steps.content: Choice C identifies the passage's three-part structure: introducing a belief, explaining its prevalence, and presenting contradictory evidence. A, B, and D misread the passage—A invents a novel methodology that doesn't appear, B frames the belief as something demonstrated rather than challenged, and D treats the research as an exception. The “But” confirms this contradictory turn is the passage's core movement. That matches choice C — C is correct.
